title: MultiworldGG world manifest
description: Validates worlds/<apworld>.json on Index-repo PRs. Karen fails the PR if a manifest does not match this schema. The apworld is the filename stem (e.g. 'oot' for 'worlds/oot.json'); it is not stored inside the document.

^_comment(_|$).description: Inline-comment fields, ignored by all consumers. Convention: '_comment_<field>' or '_comment'.

game.description: Display name shown to players. The lookup key game_name -> apworld is built from this field.

module_location.description: Source location for the world. Currently a GitHub tree URL (https://github.com/<org>/<repo>/tree/<ref>/<path>); pip-installable specs (git+ssh://, git+https://) are also valid.

world_version.description: Semver-shape version of the world implementation. Used for the .apworld vs index precedence rule (higher wins; tiebreaker: installed wheel).

world_version_full.description: Free-form descriptive version (e.g. 'alpha02i.1', '20260210-1'). Cosmetic; not parsed.

minimum_ap_version.description: Minimum AP/MultiworldGG core version this world supports. Used to gate compatibility on install.

authors.description: World authors, displayed in launcher / tracker UIs.

contributors.description: Secondary credit list. Distinct from 'authors'; not surfaced everywhere.

igdb_id.description: IGDB game id. 0 means 'no IGDB entry' (e.g. for original worlds with no commercial counterpart). The igdb-game-details workflow keys off this on PR merge.

compatible_version.description: Lowest APContainer (.apworld) schema version this world remains binary-compatible with. Canonical universal value is 7.

version.description: APContainer (.apworld) schema version this world was packaged with.

build_version.description: Build counter for the bundled artifact, when distinct from APContainer 'version'. Rarely set.

repo_url.description: Upstream repository URL when distinct from module_location (e.g. for worlds whose canonical home is outside MultiworldGG/MultiworldGG).

tracker.description: URL to a community tracker (e.g. PopTracker pack release page).

flags.description: Free-form capability flags. Consumers ignore unknown values; adding a new flag does not require a schema change.

changelog.description: Markdown lines, oldest-last. Cosmetic; not parsed.
